title: Work-order dispatch executes real work-orders — the v1 schema contract gets its engine
description:
The work-orders schema (catalog-installable, v1) declares a complete dispatch contract: typed input, context-block injection, output validation, operation-scoped capability clamp, deterministic real-checks, bounded retry. The shipped engine implements a demo slice on a dispatch primitive that cannot act: executeAgent is a single-turn completion primitive that never binds tool implementations, the loop consumes only 5 of the 9 required fields, no ceremony seeds the agents tier the loader resolves from, the sanctioned agent-authoring tool is unreachable non-interactively, and non-interactive failure is mislabeled as a human abort. This feature closes the contract-engine gap so a filed work-order runs end to end from the loop's own operating context (pi -p non-interactive included).
The dispatch architecture is decided (DEC-0022, enacted): acting work-order agents dispatch as pi subprocesses (pi --mode json), where real tools exist, and executeAgent stays the classify/structured-output primitive; work-orders are not re-scoped to output-only. Implementation follows this shape (option a of analysis/2026-07-07-work-order-dispatch-dogfood-breaks.md §cross-cutting), shipped on main and verified end to end (FGAP-124 closed).
motivation: User-directed dogfooding probe (2026-07-07): WO-001, a minimal real work-order, was driven through run-work-order-loop from pi -p. The dispatched agent received ZERO executable tools (empirically instrumented: grant ['write','bash'] passed the DEC-0047 clamp and was discarded; the child LLM got a bare prompt), so its 'cannot write files' statement was accurate; the deterministic real-check correctly failed; the retry gate silently auto-declined and recorded 'aborted-by-human'. Every honest work-order must fail this way today. Investigation of record: analysis/2026-07-07-work-order-dispatch-dogfood-breaks.md.
acceptance_criteria: The dispatch-architecture decision (subprocess vs executor tool-loop vs output-only re-scope) is filed as a decision before implementation; the implementation matches it on every surface.
A dispatched target agent can act: the WO-001 probe recipe (a work-order whose agent must write a file, verified by a runtime-demo real-check) passes end to end, non-interactively.

The engine honors every schema-required field: input validated against input_contract, the work-order's context_blocks injected, output validated against output_contract, the capability clamp intersecting wo.scope.operations — or the schema is amended to stop asserting engine behavior that was decided out, dual-surface.

A fresh substrate can produce a resolvable target_agent from the loop's operating context: the agents tier is seeded by ceremony or a bundled tier is wired, and agent authoring has a sanctioned non-interactive path or a filed caller-as-reconciler policy (FGAP-068 shape).

Non-interactive runs report honest statuses (no environment default recorded as a human decision) and carry an explicit opt-in retry policy; the gate-coverage inconsistency is closed (a composite op never embeds an effect its gated ceremony tool protects without the gate).
Regression: the probe recipe is a pinned test; the deterministic real-check verdict path stays exit-code/artifact-parsed, never LLM self-report.
